prTitle: feat: implement SettingsView, NotificationSettingsView, and SettingsViewModel
prBody:
## Summary

- **`NotificationScheduler`** — wraps `UNUserNotificationCenter` with `requestPermission()`, `scheduleReminders(days:time:)`, and `cancelAll()`; uses a `Weekday` enum (raw values matching `Calendar` weekday integers) for type-safe day selection
- **`CloudSyncService`** — detects iCloud availability via `FileManager.ubiquityIdentityToken` and publishes `CloudSyncState` (`.synced` / `.error`); `@Observable` for SwiftUI binding
- **`SettingsViewModel`** — `@Observable @MainActor` class coordinating both services; persists appearance mode, notification-enabled flag, reminder days, and reminder time to `UserDefaults` so all settings survive app termination and restore on next launch; appearance defaults to `.dark`
- **`SettingsView`** — `Form`-based settings screen with appearance `Picker` (segmented, dark default), notifications `Toggle` with conditional `NavigationLink`, and iCloud row that shows an error `HStack` banner when `syncState == .error`
- **`NotificationSettingsView`** — 7-column `WeekdayGridView` of pill buttons for multi-select day toggling and a `DatePicker` (hourAndMinute) for reminder time; changes propagate back to `SettingsViewModel` which calls `scheduleReminders`
- **`SettingsViewModelTests`** — 15 tests covering default state, appearance persistence round-trip, notification permission request, scheduling/cancellation, reminder-days/time persistence, and cloud sync state reflection

Closes #29
